title: Single-file durability and portability
description: The whole database is one file (plus WAL sidecars), which simplifies deployment but constrains backup and concurrency.
body: Use when reasoning about deployment, backup, or moving a database. SQLite stores everything in one cross-platform file, making it ideal for embedded, on-device, and single-node use, and it is portable between architectures (consistent byte order). But that file model means backup must be transactionally aware (online backup or VACUUM INTO, not a live cp), concurrency is bounded by the single-writer lock, and it should not live on a network share. Do not treat the .db as a plain blob you can copy or sync mid-write. Actions: use the backup API, keep it on local storage, and tune synchronous for your durability needs. Validation signals include a verified, integrity-checked backup. Risk boundary: putting an active SQLite file on NFS/SMB or a sync folder risks corruption.
